report.summary: A CRITICAL flood has inundated 153.4 km2 of Peshawar, impacting 540,000 people and placing 14 hospitals, 67 schools, and 89 km of roads at risk. SAR analysis confirms the event at 91% confidence with deep-water concentrations in zones FZ-01 and FZ-02. The most urgent constraint is that only one evacuation route remains viable, creating a critical logistics bottleneck. Immediate priorities are evacuating high-risk zones, securing hospital access, and clearing blocked corridors. These are decision-support estimates requiring ground-truth validation; optical assessment is limited by 42% cloud cover.
report.detailed_body: Satellite SAR analysis over Peshawar reveals a severe flood event with 153.37 km² of inundation affecting 24.3% of the monitored area. Deep water zones span 12.4 km² in Zone FZ-01, while Zone FZ-02 has 8.7 km² of high water. A population of 540,000 is impacted, with 67 schools affected, 89 km of roads blocked, and 14 hospitals at risk, including Lady Reading Hospital under high risk. Only one evacuation route remains viable, severely constraining response. The combination of extensive flooding, critical infrastructure at risk, and limited safe passage creates a CRITICAL emergency situation demanding immediate, large-scale intervention.
report.technical_analysis: Sentinel-1 SAR was selected because cloud cover is 42%. The analysis uses SAR VV/VH ratio with mean value 0.24 and identifies 22 zones. Damage is estimated at 24.3%, with 89 km of roads blocked and a vulnerability score of 8.2.
recommendations: Prioritize evacuation in critical flood zones.
Deploy emergency medical support near hospitals at risk.
Clear blocked road corridors for rescue access.
Open temporary shelters for displaced families.
Monitor flood expansion using updated satellite imagery.
Evacuate high-risk flood zones FZ-01 and FZ-02
Secure access to Lady Reading Hospital
Establish emergency traffic control on the single evacuation route
Water rescue boats and amphibious vehicles
response_priorities: Activate evacuation support for FZ-01 and FZ-02.
Protect hospital access and stage medical surge teams near high-risk facilities.
Clear blocked road corridors needed for rescue and logistics movement.
assumptions: Satellite, hazard, and impact values are local demo data until upstream agents publish live outputs.
Risk zone geometries are simplified mock polygons for dashboard and report demonstration.
Facility and route coordinates are approximate and intended for prototype visualization.
limitations: No live Band messages are consumed in this local demo run.
No field validation, hydrologic model, or live basemap tiles are used for artifact generation.
Impact estimates should be treated as decision-support indicators, not official counts.

criticality.rationale: CRITICAL flood confirmed via SAR (91% confidence). 540,000 people affected, 14 hospitals at risk, 89 km roads blocked, and only 1 evacuation route remains. Mass casualties likely without immediate escalation.
trigger_factors: 14 hospitals at risk including Lady Reading (HIGH)

anomalies.description: Evacuation route count (1) is critically low relative to affected population (540,000)
anomalies.recommended_handling: Flag as operational bottleneck for emergency logistics

map_narrative.map_narrative: The static risk map identifies a critical flood emergency in Peshawar, covering 153.37 km² (24.3% of the area). Using SAR imagery to penetrate cloud cover, the map highlights severe inundation across 22 zones, impacting 540,000 people and creating a critical logistics bottleneck with only one remaining evacuation route.
key_spatial_findings: 24.3% of the monitored area is inundated
89 km of road networks are blocked, severely restricting mobility
High vulnerability score (8.2) driven by concentrations of affected schools and hospitals
Single viable evacuation route creates a high-risk bottleneck for mass movement
hotspots: Flood Zones FZ-01 and FZ-02 (deep-water concentrations)
map_limitations: Satellite-derived estimates require ground-truth field validation
Optical visibility limited by 42% cloud cover, necessitating reliance on SAR data

coordination_priorities: Manage evacuation bottleneck to prevent traffic gridlock

decision_brief.decision_brief: CRITICAL flood emergency in Peshawar, Pakistan confirmed via Sentinel-1 SAR at 91% confidence. 153.4 km2 (24.3% of monitored area) inundated across 22 zones, affecting 540,000 people, 14 hospitals, 67 schools, and blocking 89 km of roads. Only one viable evacuation route remains, creating a severe mass-movement bottleneck. Escalation is required and immediate action is needed to prevent mass casualties. Estimates are satellite-derived and require field validation.
decision_brief.official_summary: A CRITICAL flood has inundated 153.4 km2 of Peshawar, impacting 540,000 people and placing 14 hospitals, 67 schools, and 89 km of roads at risk. SAR analysis confirms the event at 91% confidence with deep-water concentrations in zones FZ-01 and FZ-02. The most urgent constraint is that only one evacuation route remains viable, creating a critical logistics bottleneck. Immediate priorities are evacuating high-risk zones, securing hospital access, and clearing blocked corridors. These are decision-support estimates requiring ground-truth validation; optical assessment is limited by 42% cloud cover.
key_decisions_required: Authorize immediate evacuation of high-risk flood zones FZ-01 and FZ-02
Establish emergency traffic control to manage the single evacuation route and prevent gridlock
Deploy medical surge teams and secure access to Lady Reading and Khyber Teaching Hospitals
Mobilize heavy machinery and water rescue assets to clear 89 km of blocked roads
Open temporary shelters for displaced residents and initiate field validation of SAR estimates
Resolve missing report/map document links and verify generation pipeline
